news.title: Haryana RERA directs Imperia Structures to pay ₹55 lakh compensation to homebuyers
news.para: Haryana RERA has ordered Imperia Structures to pay homebuyers ₹55 lakh for lost property appreciation. The builder must also compensate for mental agony and litigation costs. Homebuyers booked an apartment in Gurugram's 'The Esfera' project in 2013. Possession was promised by May 2017 but was delayed. The authority previously ordered a refund of ₹73.37 lakh.

news.title: India’s flex office stock crosses 100 million sq ft, demand driven by IT, GCCs: Report
news.para: Crossing the 100 million sq ft mark, India's flexible office space has witnessed remarkable expansion in just five years. At the helm is Bengaluru, boasting the highest concentration of flexible work environments. The surge in demand is primarily fueled by IT, technology, and BFSI industries, with international corporations taking the lead in space absorption.

news.title: ASK Property Fund exits ₹650 crore investment in Mahagun’s Noida project
news.para: ASK Property Fund has successfully exited its investment in a Mahagun Group residential project in Noida. The fund generated a 21% IRR and a 2.1x multiple from this deal. Additionally, ASK Property Fund completed a full exit of its 2018 debt fund, achieving an investment multiple of 1.83x.

news.title: Telangana HC pulls up HYDRAA for taking over plot in Hayathnagar
news.para: The Hyderabad High Court has again criticized HYDRAA for allegedly seizing a 650 square yard plot, deeming the action unconstitutional. Justice NV Shravan Kumar expressed shock at the agency's interference in sub judice property disputes, noting a lack of change in HYDRAA's attitude despite previous court orders. The court has sought a response from HYDRAA by March 25.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh: UPSIDA approves eight new warehousing, logistics projects
news.para: Uttar Pradesh State Industrial Development Authority has approved eight new projects in warehousing and logistics. These projects represent an investment of around Rs 800 crore. The approvals are part of the Uttar Pradesh Warehousing and Logistics Policy-2022. The new projects include silos, warehousing units, and a logistics project. They will be developed across several districts.

news.title: Hyderabad civic body seals Katriya Hotel and Towers over ₹6 crore property tax dues
news.para: Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) has sealed Katriya Hotel and Towers for outstanding property tax dues of Rs 6.16 crore, defaulting since 2017. This action is part of a broader enforcement drive against major defaulters, with five other properties also sealed. GHMC urges prompt payment, highlighting a 90% interest waiver.

news.title: Pune: PCMC earns ₹80 crore in property tax through amnesty scheme, likely to miss fiscal target
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation's property tax amnesty scheme, offering a 90% penalty waiver, has seen a strong response, collecting nearly Rs 80 crore in its initial 20 days. Despite this boost, overall collections are projected to fall short of the Rs 1,000 crore target and may not match last year's figures.

news.title: Embassy Developments’ subsidiary faces KIADB order to surrender 78-acre Bengaluru land
news.para: Embassy East Business Park has been ordered by the Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board to surrender 78 acres of land in Bengaluru within 30 days. KIADB alleges breach of agreement terms, citing unauthorized sub-lease arrangements. EEBP denies the allegations, stating it obtained necessary no-objection certificates and plans to pursue legal remedies.

news.title: BMC asks MMRDA & MHADA to settle ₹2,287 crore property tax dues
news.para: The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has demanded over Rs 2,287 crore in property tax from the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) and Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority (Mhada). These dues are critical for funding civic services and infrastructure projects across Mumbai. The BMC is actively pursuing recovery to strengthen its financial position.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh clears private business park policy to woo global firms
news.para: Uttar Pradesh's cabinet has approved the Private Business Park Development Scheme-2025, aiming to attract global companies and R&D centers with world-class plug-and-play infrastructure. This policy will expedite project setup, boost investment, and generate thousands of jobs, particularly in the services and technology sectors. Developed under a DBFOT model, these parks will feature a 45-year concession period for private developers.

news.title: India Accelerator to invest $5 million to double footprint in next 12-18 months
news.para: India Accelerator is set to invest $5 million to grow its coworking spaces across India. The company plans to double its presence in cities within the next 12 to 18 months. New centers are planned for Vadodara, Kolkata, Guwahati, and Bengaluru by the first half of 2026. This expansion aims to enhance community and coworking facilities nationwide.

news.title: Lucknow civic body's budget 2026 okays no hike in property tax
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation approved a Rs 1,253 crore budget for 2026-27, focusing on development without increasing property tax or parking charges. The civic body aims to boost revenue through better tax collection and expanding the tax base, with funds allocated for infrastructure improvements, sanitation, and beautification projects across all wards.

news.title: Over 500 objects to proposed collector guideline rates for property in Indore
news.para: Nearly 500 objections were filed against proposed collector guideline rate hikes in Indore, with citizens and experts deeming increases unrealistic. Concerns include blanket hikes for bypass-adjacent land, outdated apartment valuations, and prohibitive costs for villages near new road projects. The District Evaluation Committee will review these complaints before forwarding the final proposal to Bhopal.

news.title: Haryana rights body seeks report on death of seven workers at Gurugram construction site collapse
news.para: The Haryana Human Rights Commission has issued notices to officials following the death of seven workers in a Gurugram construction site collapse. The commission is seeking reports on safety compliance, accountability, and compensation, emphasizing the need for stricter enforcement of labour safety laws to protect workers' fundamental human rights.

news.title: Allotment of plots in new industrial areas of Indore region to begin from April
news.para: The Madhya Pradesh Industrial Development Corporation will begin allotting plots in nine new industrial areas across the Indore region from April. These developments aim to attract investments in sectors like food processing, confectionery, and plastics, bolstering MSME-driven industries. The expansion includes a large township at Bardari and several smaller clusters to support business growth.

news.title: Jaipur development body unveils five land pooling schemes
news.para: The Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) has proposed five new land pooling schemes to support urban expansion, including industrial, residential, and mixed-use developments. These plans span areas along Mahal Road, Agra Road, Kalwar Road, and near Tonk Road, covering significant land parcels for diverse development needs.

news.title: Supreme Universal to invest ₹1,500 crore to develop residential project in Pune
news.para: Supreme Universal is set to invest ₹1,500 crore in a new residential project in Pune's Punawale. The development, named Supreme Rivana, will span 12.6 acres and offer over 2,200 apartments.  Construction will occur in phases, with the investment covering all building costs.
